<!--
Thanks for contributing! Keep PRs small and focused — one resource (or one fix) per PR merges fastest.
Delete the sections that don't apply.
-->

## What does this PR do?

<!-- One or two sentences. e.g. "Adds <tool> to the Runtimes section." -->

## Type of change

- [ ] Add a resource (runtime, tool, benchmark, paper, article)
- [ ] Fix a stale / broken / redirected link
- [ ] Correct or add a benchmark figure
- [ ] Docs / wording / formatting
- [ ] Other (describe above)

## Inclusion checklist (for new resources)

<!-- Skip if this is a fix or docs-only change. -->

- [ ] It is **CPU-native or CPU-first** — CPU is a primary target, not a slow fallback.
- [ ] It is **directly relevant** to the CPU-vs-GPU inference decision.
- [ ] It is **maintained** (activity in the last ~12 months), or I noted its archived status in the description.
- [ ] It is **not a duplicate** of an existing entry.
- [ ] The description is **one line, in my own words** — neutral and specific, not marketing copy.
- [ ] The entry is placed in the correct section and keeps that section's ordering.

## Evidence / notes

<!-- Benchmark numbers, a link to CPU deployment docs, or why this beats what's already listed.
     Per CONTRIBUTING.md, unverifiable numbers get a `<!-- TODO: cite benchmark -->` placeholder rather than a made-up figure. -->
